original_abstract:
Diffusion Imaging in Python (Dipy) is a free, open-source software library dedicated to the analysis of diffusion magnetic resonance imaging (dMRI) data. As a specialized application of MRI, dMRI allows researchers to investigate the microscopic structural features of brain white matter. By modeling the local configuration of nerve fiber bundles, dMRI can be used to infer the complex trajectories of pathways that connect distant regions of the brain. Dipy provides a comprehensive suite of tools that span the entire dMRI analysis pipeline: from signal pre-processing and the reconstruction of diffusion distributions within individual voxels to fiber tractography, post-processing, and 3D visualization. 

Dipy is designed to offer transparent, high-level implementations of both established and cutting-edge algorithms through a uniform programming interface. These include classical techniques—such as the diffusion tensor model and deterministic tractography—alongside advanced methods like constrained spherical deconvolution, diffusion spectrum imaging (DSI) with deconvolution, probabilistic tracking, and original routines for tractography clustering. To support further research and development, the library also includes essential utility functions for statistical analysis, informative data visualization, and flexible file handling. 

Unlike many scientific software packages developed within a single laboratory, Dipy is an international, community-driven project. It encourages open collaboration between scientists and developers via GitHub and public mailing lists. This inclusive model has fostered a growing global team of contributors from diverse academic institutions across five countries and three continents, ensuring that Dipy remains at the forefront of neuroinformatics.
humanized_abstract:
Diffusion Imaging in Python (Dipy) is a freely available open-source library for analysis of diffusion magnetic resonance imaging (dMRI) data. MRI is an imaging technique that provides information on the physical environment at the scale of few microns. Diffusion MRI (dMRI) is a specific type of MRI that can quantify microscopic structure of brain white matter, and can be used to model locally the architecture of bundles of nerve fibers, and hence infer the main pathways (tracks) connecting distant brain regions. Dipy is an open-source software library that comprises a broad range of tools to cover almost every stage of the processing of dMRI data, from early signal processing and estimation of diffusion distributions in each voxel, to subsequent steps such as fiber reconstruction, additional processing and 3D visualization.

Dipy is providing transparent, high-level implementations of common and state of the art processing algorithms, all within a consistent interface. This includes classical algorithms such as diffusion tensor model and deterministic methods, as well as more recent methods such as constrained spherical deconvolution, diffusion spectrum imaging (DSI) with associated deconvolution, probabilistic, as well as some original methods for tractography clustering. In addition, Dipy also includes some utility functions for further research and development needs, ranging from basic statistical operations to more advanced data representation and visualization as well as flexible file handling.

While most scientific software packages are the product of a single lab, Dipy is an international, open-source effort to gather contributions from researchers and programmers all over the world. We have a page on GitHub and several public mailing lists to facilitate this collaborative effort. We now have a dynamic and global team of contributors from many universities across five countries and three continents, turning Dipy into a truly active and competitive neuroinformatics project.
